project.name: Reel Scripter
project.description: A minimalist, high-performance web application designed for scripting short-form video content (Instagram Reels, TikToks). It streamlines the creative workflow from initial drafting to filming and archiving, providing a distraction-free environment with integrated AI powered feedback loops for optimizing hooks and intros.
project.tech_summary: Next.js 16.1.6 (App Router), React 19.2.3, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Drizzle ORM, PostgreSQL

user_workflow.primary_flow: User authenticates (password) → Dashboard (Write tab) → Creates New Script → Editor Interface → Drafts Body & Context → Defines Intros (Hooks/Verbal Intros) → Requests AI Analysis via Side-Car Drawer → Reviews & Applies AI Suggestions → Marks as 'Filmed' → Dashboard (Edit tab) → Final Polish → Marks as 'Done' → Dashboard (Archive tab)
user_workflow.user_goal: To rapidly iterate on short-form video concepts, leveraging AI for optimization, and managing the production status of multiple scripts in a unified workspace.
key_interactions: Dashboard: Filtering scripts by status (Write, Edit, Archive) using URL parameters
Editor: Real-time drafting with auto-save operations (via server actions)
AI Feedback: Opening the Suggestion Drawer, triggering n8n webhooks for 'Hook' or 'Intro' analysis
Context Management: Adding research notes/context that feed into the AI analysis context window
Status Management: Visual toggles to move scripts through the production pipeline (Draft -> Filmed -> Done -> Archived)

architecture.overview: A server-centric Next.js 16 application leveraging React Server Components for data hydration and Server Actions for all mutations. The architecture prioritizes simplicity and direct database interaction, eschewing a separate API layer in favor of collocated data access patterns.
architecture.data_flow: Server Page fetches data (Drizzle) → Hydrates Client Component (Editor) → User Interaction → Server Action (mutation) → Database Update → revalidatePath() → Client UI Update

key_components.purpose: Central hub for script management. Implements tab-based filtering and status visualization.
key_components.interactions: Fetches scripts based on searchParams, handles creation of new scripts via form action.

key_components.purpose: Primary workspace. Manages local state for immediate feedback while syncing with server.
key_components.interactions: Invokes 'updateScript' for content changes, manages 'SuggestionDrawer' visibility state.

key_components.purpose: Side-car interface for AI interactions. Displays analysis history and allows content replacement.
key_components.interactions: Calls 'generateSuggestion' (n8n link) and 'applySuggestion' actions. Uses useTransition for pending states.

key_components.purpose: Business logic and database interface. Handles auth, CRUD operations, and external API calls.
key_components.interactions: Bridge between client components and Postgres/N8n. Enforces 'Scorched Earth' strategy for sub-items.

entry_points.purpose: Root route (Dashboard). Handles authentication check (middleware) and script listing.
Script Detail Route. Fetches script + relations (intros, contextItems) for the Editor.

entry_points.trigger: Form submissions and event handlers in Client Components

design_decisions: Direct Database Access in Server Components: Reduces latency and complexity by removing the API serialization layer.
Scorched Earth Update Strategy: For 'Context Items', updates delete all existing records for a script and re-insert active ones to avoid complex diffing logic.
Force-Dynamic Rendering: Pages explicitly export `const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` to prevent build-time static generation failures with database connections.
External AI Processing: AI logic is offloaded to n8n webhooks, keeping the Next.js backend lightweight and focused on orchestration.

core.name: Next.js 16.1.6
core.why: Cutting-edge RSC support and Server Actions for simplified state management

core.name: React 19.2.3
core.why: Required for Next.js 16, provides useTransition and optimized hydration

core.name: Tailwind CSS v4
core.why: Zero-runtime CSS with modern utility features via @tailwindcss/postcss

core.name: Drizzle ORM
core.why: TypeScript-first SQL builder with zero runtime overhead compared to heavy ORMs

dependencies.name: clsx / tailwind-merge
dependencies.purpose: Safe class name concatenation and conflict resolution

tech_stack.infrastructure: PostgreSQL (Neon/Railway), Vercel/Railway for hosting, N8n for AI workflows

file_organization.philosophy: Feature-based organization within `app` directory, with shared UI components in `components` and data/logic in `lib` and `db`.

key_directories.contains: Route definitions (page.tsx), layouts, and Server Actions

important_files.role: Contains all mutation logic: login, createScript, updateScript, generateSuggestion
Complex client-side editor with auto-save and tab management
Drizzle definitions for scripts, intros, context_items, and suggestions

conventions.naming: PascalCase for Components, camelCase for logic/variables, kebab-case for directories
conventions.structure: Colocation of route-specific logic where possible, strictly typed interfaces
conventions.imports: Use of `@/` path aliases for all internal imports

development_guide.working_with_this_codebase: Focus on `app/actions.ts` for backend logic and `components/Editor.tsx` for the primary UI. The data flow relies heavily on referencing database IDs (UUIDs) across relations.
key_patterns: Server Actions must validate inputs before DB operations.
Client components should use `useTransition` when calling Server Actions to show loading states.
All pages interacting with the DB must be `dynamic = 'force-dynamic'`.
AI features depend on valid n8n webhook usage; check `actions.ts` for endpoints.

common_tasks.task: Adding a new field to Scripts
common_tasks.approach: 1. Add to `db/schema.ts` 2. Run `npx drizzle-kit push` 3. Update `createScript`/`updateScript` in `actions.ts` 4. Update interactions in `Editor.tsx`
Prompts are managed externally in the n8n workflow. To change behavior, update the n8n node config for the webhooks defined in `actions.ts`.

critical_rules: Ensure UUIDs are correctly handled in `updateScript` to prevent orphan records.
Do not modify the `force-dynamic` export in `page.tsx`; it is required for deployment stability.
Maintain the separate `intros` and `contextItems` relation management strategy (delete-insert vs upsert) as defined in `actions.ts`.
gotchas: The 'Scorched Earth' strategy in `updateScript` deletes all context items and re-inserts them. This means stable IDs for context items are not guaranteed across saves.
n8n Webhook URLs are hardcoded in `actions.ts`; ensure these are reachable from the production environment.
